title: verbascum × sipiadense
intro: Verbascum × sipiadense, also known as the hybrid mullein, is a rare and sterile hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Verbascum phoeniceum and Verbascum blattaria. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids, saponins, and mucilages in its leaves and flowers. The health benefits of Verbascum × sipiadense include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to act as a natural remedy for skin irritations and wounds. Verbascum × sipiadense can be prepared as a herbal infusion, tincture, or poultice to harness its medicinal properties.
